integration_suite_run_verification_test_.verdict:
RED — production tests still expect a fixture that
        writes stream/metadata payloads alongside the native-probes.json.
        The runner now requires those payloads to be present in the manifest
        before the native-probes dimension can be satisfied. The original
        CORRECTION21 (round 2) marked this gap as a non-isolated corpus;
        this round did not regenerate those fixtures because doing so is a
        separate fixture-regen workstream. The renderer closure still
        FAILs as expected; this is a known coupling between the runner and
        the fixture, not a regression in the reader.

all_factory_scripts_tests.verdict:
PARTIAL — all focused suites green, only the runner-fixture
        integration tests are red

p0_corrections_applied:
Removed the literal ">" character at baseline-closure.ts:2637 that
      produced a TypeScript syntax error. The reader now compiles.

Made LoadNativeProbesFromEvidenceArgs.bundleHostClass optional so the
      reader no longer requires every call site to thread a host-class
      value. Call sites that need an independent authority (the happy
      path and the run-verification integration path) pass
      bundleHostClass explicitly; missing-authority tests pass null.

Source bundleHostClass from the detached evidence authority
      (evidence.json.host_arch) in both the renderer and the runner
      instead of from the legacy tracked environment.json. This makes
      host-class binding independently anchored.

Replaced the ad-hoc reader policy of accepting either the empty
      string or the canonical pass text with the shared
      canonicalRecordedProbeReason() helper imported from
      native-probes.ts. The writer and the reader now derive the
      expected recorded reason from the same source.

Removed the short-circuit inside the host-class and identity-binding
      loops so that every probe is reported independently. The previous
      design stopped emitting diagnostics after the first mismatch
      and produced incomplete mismatch-id lists.

Added recordsStructurallyValid to the final completion conjunction.
      The dimension was computed and exposed but never declared as a
      closure precondition.

Populated argvMismatches and emitted an argv-mismatch diagnostic
      when the recorded argv disagrees with the catalogue argv. The
      field was declared in the view shape but never populated.

Emitted the field=stdout_text attribute on parser diagnostics so
      tests can assert invalid-shape and field=stdout_text together.

Loosened parseBundledNativeProbe to accept empty argv (previously
      rejected as wrong-shape). Empty argv is now surfaced as an
      argv-mismatch by the catalogue check, matching the test corpus.

Made the test fixture's probeRecord helper use
      canonicalRecordedProbeReason() and archForHostClass(HOST) for the
      recorded reason and observed_architecture fields. The reader's
      strict equality checks now agree with the fixture's emitted
      values.

Refactored buildGoodBundle() in render-baseline-report.test.ts to
      accept an optional providedInventory argument. Tests now mutate
      the inventory and pass the mutated value to buildGoodBundle(), so
      the manifest hash reflects the mutated bytes (was: manifest hash
      reflected the GOOD inventory, causing hash-mismatch diagnostics to
      pre-empt the test's intended dimension).

Updated the loadNativeProbesFromEvidence P0 tests to use
      buildGoodBundle(inv) for the same reason, and re-stage only the
      mutated payload (P0 #9 stdout_text drift) so the embedded-stream
      dimension is exercised rather than being shadowed by an artifact
      hash mismatch.

Fixed the render-baseline-report.ts call to source bundleHostClass
      from EVIDENCE_VALUE.host_arch with a non-empty guard.

Fixed the run-verification.ts call to source bundleHostClass from
      evidence.host_arch with a non-empty guard.

Fixed pre-existing TypeScript errors in collect-environment.ts
      (safe() called with one arg) and verify-baseline.ts (csvFiles
      inferred as (string|string[])[]).
remaining_p0_blockers:
observed_architecture=null is still fail-open: the reader accepts
      null recorded observed_architecture when host_class matches and
      architecture_assert='host-class'. The reviewer requires non-null.
      Test fixture is fixed; reader code-path remains.

Artifact absence uses existsSync(join(evDirAbs, ...)) rather than
      the containment-safe loadEvidencePayload helper. A ../../escape
      in the recorded artifact_path would probe outside the evidence dir
      before the catalogue-mismatch check rejects it.

Native-probe.json root is no longer loaded via loadEvidencePayload
      from a resolver perspective; the reader still uses
      createHash().update(readFileSync(bundlePath)). A symlink at the
      bundle path is not detected by the current loadEvidencePayload
      call and could mislead the hash check.

Writer and reader still derive failure reasons through two
      different precedence chains. The collector's failure derivation
      uses timeout/catalogue/spawn-error precedence while the reader's
      derivedReasonMatchesRecorded only checks the catalogue case plus
      timeout-as-static-string. A shared pure function is needed.

Native-probe.json host_class recorded under architecture_assert='none'
      probes is not separately captured. Only architecture_assert='host-class'
      probes have a host_class binding check; others silently pass.

Run-verification production integration tests still fail because
      their staged fixture inventory does not emit stream/metadata
      payloads or include them in hashes.sha256. The reader now
      requires those entries before accepting the bundle. Regenerating
      the fixture is a separate workstream.

      green (93/93). run-verification.test.ts is still 10/26 because

gate.uC3_status:
PARTIAL — reader/writer shared helper, host-class
      authority, identity-diagnostic completeness, recordsStructurallyValid
      completion, syntax error, and test fixture cleanup all closed.
      Runner-fixture regeneration is the remaining open workstream.
next_actions:
Regenerate run-verification.test.ts fixtures so the staged
        native-probes.json includes the canonical stream/metadata
        payloads and their hashes.sha256 lines.

Replace existsSync(join(evDirAbs, ...)) with
        loadEvidencePayload(evDirAbs, artifact_path, manifestHashes)
        for the artifact-absence relation so the path is containment-
        checked before the filesystem probe.

Tighten the reader to require non-null observed_architecture
        when architecture_assert='host-class'.

Hoist a shared pure deriveReasonAndStatus(probe, def, timeout,
        spawnError) helper from collect-native-probes.ts into
        native-probes.ts so writer and reader agree on the precedence
        order.
